items.title: Personalized digital tools boost smoking quit rates
items.content: This network meta-analysis of 152 randomized controlled trials found that personalized and group-customized digital smoking-cessation interventions — particularly text messages and app-based tools — significantly improved cessation outcomes, as compared with standard care. These interventions demonstrated greatest efficacy in middle-aged adults and short-to-medium-term programmes. The work provides a foundation for future digital smoking-cessation frameworks.

items.title: Publisher Correction: Representational dynamics during extinction of fear memories in the human brain
items.author: Nikolai Axmacher
items.content: Publisher Correction: Representational dynamics during extinction of fear memories in the human brain

items.title: Quantifying the impact of hair and skin characteristics on fNIRS signal quality for enhanced inclusivity
items.author: David A. Boas
items.content: Yücel et al. examine how skin and hair characteristics impact functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) signal quality and provide recommendations for inclusive use of fNIRS.

items.title: Efficacy of digital interventions for smoking cessation by type and method: a systematic review and network meta-analysis
items.author: Xuelei Ma
items.content: A network meta-analysis of 152 randomized controlled trials finds that personalized and group-customized digital interventions, especially SMS and app-based tools, improve smoking cessation versus standard care, with greater benefits among middle-aged adults in short- to medium-term programmes.

items.title: Decoupling geographical constraints from human mobility
items.author: Sune Lehmann
items.content: Boucherie et al. apply physics-based models to the arrangement of locations to study how geography shapes human movement. They find an underlying pattern in how people choose to move, independent of geographical layout.

items.title: Spousal correlations for nine psychiatric disorders are consistent across cultures and persistent over generations
items.author: Shi-Heng Wang
items.content: A study of millions of couples shows spouses often share psychiatric disorders. This pattern is consistent across cultures (Taiwan and Nordic countries) and has persisted for generations, impacting how these disorders are inherited.

items.title: Complementary, alternative and integrative medicine for autism: an umbrella review and online platform
items.author: Richard Delorme
items.content: The use of complementary, alternative and integrative medicine (CAIM) is highly prevalent among autistic individuals. This umbrella review finds that there is no high-quality evidence to support the efficacy of any CAIM for core or associated symptoms of autism. Although several CAIMs showed promising results, they were supported by very low-quality evidence.

items.title: A latent measure of cultural racism and its association with US mortality and life expectancy
items.author: Nancy Krieger
items.content: Akinyemiju et al. develop and validate a theory-driven, empirical measure of cultural racism and identify its associations with public health disparities in the USA, including a positive association with mortality and a negative association with life expectancy.
